title: Dividend Discount Model: If a firm’s current stock price is $40, and it...
summary: Calculate stock price using the Gordon Growth Model with dividend, growth rate, and required return.
prompt: If a firm’s current stock price is $40, and it is expected to pay a dividend of $2 next year, what is the expected growth rate if the required rate of return is 10%?
solution: Using the Gordon Growth Model: P0 = D1 / (r - g) => g = r - (D1 / P0) = 10% - (2 / 40) = 10% - 5% = 5%.
